extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won Stage 15 in a reduced uphill sprint, holding off ACKERMANN Pascal by a bike length after MØRKØV Michael's late leadout.
tactical_patterns: MØRKØV Michael leadout faded late uphill, forcing ACKERMANN Pascal offline while PHILIPSEN Jasper held straighter trajectory—key advantage in gradient finishes.
13-rider breakaway collapsed despite 230km stage and headwind—Movistar controlled without committing full teams, revealing peloton's sprint interest threshold.
MARTIN Guillaume abandoned KOM collection late despite commanding lead—headwind and rain made energy preservation trump jersey math with 51pt buffer.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: won uphill sprint by holding tighter line than ACKERMANN after teammate leadout faded, shows tactical maturity in positioning
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: forced wide around fading MØRKØV, lost momentum on gradient—second place shows speed but poor positioning on technical finishes
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: neutral: leadout work for UAE (PHILIPSEN) effective until final 100m where gradient exposure showed—not his own sprinter
rider_signals.MARTIN Guillaume: positive: secured near-unassailable KOM lead (85pts, 51pt gap) then conserved energy in rain—mature resource management
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: retained 39sec GC lead passively while Jumbo sheltered him—commentary notes he's hurt but managing losses effectively
